deckard
  • deckard
  • Data
  • View page source

Data

The deckard.data module defines the DataConfig dataclass, which provides a unified interface for loading, generating, preprocessing, and splitting datasets for machine learning experiments. It supports both real and synthetic datasets, as well as YAML/Hydra-based configuration.

class deckard.data.DataConfig(_target_='deckard.utils.ConfigBase', dataset_name='adult', data_params=None, test_size=0.2, random_state=42, stratify=True)[source]

Bases: ConfigBase

Configuration and utility class for loading, preprocessing, and splitting datasets for machine learning tasks.

dataset_name

Name of the dataset to load or path to a data file.

Type:

str

data_params

Additional parameters for data loading or generation.

Type:

dict

test_size

Proportion of the dataset to include in the test split (between 0 and 1).

Type:

float

random_state

Seed for random number generation to ensure reproducibility.

Type:

int

stratify

Specifies stratification for sampling; can be None, True (use target), or a column name.

Type:

Union[None, str, bool]

_X

Loaded feature matrix.

Type:

pd.DataFrame

_y

Loaded target vector.

Type:

pd.Series

_data_load_time

Time taken to load the data.

Type:

float

_data_sample_time

Time taken to sample/split the data.

Type:

float

_train_indices

Indices for training samples.

Type:

list

_test_indices

Indices for testing samples.

Type:

list

_X_train

Training feature matrix.

Type:

pd.DataFrame

_y_train

Training target vector.

Type:

pd.Series

_X_test

Testing feature matrix.

Type:

pd.DataFrame

_y_test

Testing target vector.

Type:

pd.Series

score_dict

Dictionary to store scores or metrics.

Type:

dict

_target_

Internal identifier for the class.

Type:

str

__post_init__()[source]

Post-initialization method to validate parameters and initialize internal attributes.

__hash__()[source]

Computes a hash value for the instance based on non-private attributes.

_load_adult_income_data()[source]

Loads and preprocesses the Adult Income dataset from OpenML.

_load_diabetes_data()[source]

Loads and preprocesses the diabetes dataset from scikit-learn.

_load_digits_data()[source]

Loads and preprocesses the digits dataset from scikit-learn.

_make_classification_data()[source]

Generates a synthetic classification dataset.

_make_regression_data()[source]

Generates a synthetic regression dataset.

_sample()[source]

Splits the loaded dataset into training and testing sets, optionally using stratification.

_load_data()[source]

Loads the dataset based on the specified name or file type.

__call__(filepath=None)[source]

Loads and samples the dataset, splits it into training and testing sets, and returns the corresponding features and labels.

save(filepath)

Saves the current state of the DataConfig instance to a file.

load(filepath)

Loads the state of the DataConfig instance from a file.

Raises:

  • ValueError – For invalid parameter values or missing data.

  • NotImplementedError – For unsupported datasets or file types.

Examples

config = DataConfig(dataset_name=”adult”, **kwargs) config() X_train = config.X_train y_train = config.y_train X_test = config.X_test y_test = config.y_test score_dict = config.score_dict

data_params: dict = None
dataset_name: str = 'adult'
random_state: int = 42
stratify: Union[None, str, bool] = True
test_size: float = 0.2
deckard.data.data_main(args=None)[source]
Parameters:

  • args (argparse.Namespace, optional) – Parsed command-line arguments. If None, arguments are parsed from sys.argv.

  • validation. (Main function for data initialization and)

  • arguments (Parses command-line)

  • logging (sets up)

  • configuration (loads data)

Return type:

None

:param : :param and validates the consistency of training and testing datasets.: :param Steps: :param —–: :param 1. Parses known arguments for data file path.: :param 2. Sets up logging at INFO level.: :param 3. Loads data configuration using initialize_data_config.: :param 4. Loads data from the specified file path.: :param 5. Extracts training and testing features: :param labels: :param and optional attributes.: :param 6. Asserts that feature and label arrays have matching lengths for both train and test sets.: :param 7. Logs the sizes of the training and testing sets.:

Raises:

  • AssertionError – If any of the data arrays have mismatched lengths.

  • Logs –

  • ---- –

  • Train and test set sizes. –

deckard.data.initialize_data_config()[source]

Initializes the data configuration using command-line arguments.

Parses known arguments for data configuration file and parameters, then initializes the DataConfig instance using initialize_config.

Returns:

An instance of DataConfig initialized with the specified configuration.

Return type:

DataConfig

Overview

DataConfig can load well-known datasets such as:

  • Adult Income (via OpenML)

  • Diabetes and Digits (from scikit-learn)

  • Synthetic datasets via make_classification or make_regression

  • CSV files that contain a target column

It also supports reproducible splits via train_test_split with optional stratification, timing instrumentation, and hashing for config tracking.

Usage

Command-line example

Run data setup directly from the terminal:

# Load the Adult dataset with default settings
python -m deckard data --data_config_file configs/data/adult.yaml

# Override configuration parameters inline
python -m deckard.data --data_params dataset_name=make_classification test_size=0.25

Programmatic usage

Use DataConfig from within your Python scripts or notebooks:

from deckard.data import initialize_data_config

# Initialize using default, Hydra/YAML, or CLI configuration
data = initialize_data_config(dataset_name="adult", test_size=0.2, random_state=42)

# Load and split the dataset
data(data_filepath="data.pkl", score_file="data_scores.json")
X_train = data.X_train
X_test = data.X_test
y_train = data.y_train
y_test = data.y_test

print(f"Train size: {len(X_train)} | Test size: {len(X_test)}")

Custom configuration

You can define a YAML file or override config parameters inline.

Example minimal YAML (adult.yaml):

_target_: data.DataConfig
dataset_name: adult
test_size: 0.2
random_state: 42
stratify: True

Example inline overrides:

python -m deckard.data --data_params dataset_name=make_classification n_samples=2000 n_features=20

Internals

Timing and logging

The data loading and splitting process is timed, and the duration is stored in the _data_load_time and _data_sample_time attributes of the DataConfig instance. This can be useful for comparing the run-time efficiency of different datasets of various methods. Logging is performed at key steps.

Troubleshooting

If you encounter issues with dataset loading, ensure that: - You have an active internet connection for datasets fetched from OpenML. - The specified CSV file path is correct and the file is accessible. - Otherwise, use one of the built-in datasets or synthetic data generation options.
